data.title: Bitcoin's value drops by 22%, raising concerns about possible worst Q1 performance since 2018. In the red for Jan and Feb.
data.description: Bitcoin is on track to record a loss at the end of this month, which would signify a historic occurrence for the cryptocurrency. This would be the first time Bitcoin ends both January and February in negative territory.

data.title: Crypto winter ending soon, according to Tom Lee. BTC may find support at $60,000, ETH at $1,890. April possible end.
data.description: Chairman Tom Lee of BitMine Immersion Technologies Inc. (NYSE: BMNR) indicated that the "cryptocurrency winter" is approaching its conclusion, with Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) and Ethereum (CRYPTO: ETH) on the verge of reaching their lowest points. During an interview, Lee referred to advice from Tom DeMark, an advisor at BitMine, who projected that Bitcoin could stabilize around $60,000 and Ethereum could reach a low of approximately $1,890. Lee stated that Bitcoin nearly reached this level, and according to DeMark, may need to dip slightly below before rebounding. This signals that the end of the downturn is imminent. Lee, co-founder of Fundstrat, suggested that the crypto winter might conclude by April, if it hasn't already. For more details, refer to the complete coverage on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Kevin O'Leary awarded $2.8M in defamation case against Ben Armstrong by Miami judge due to lack of response.
data.description: In a legal case in Miami, a default judgment has been issued by a federal judge against Ben Armstrong for failing to respond to a complaint filed by Kevin O’Leary. Armstrong was accused of defamation in the complaint. The specific monetary or punitive damages were not mentioned in the provided information.

data.title: Russian officials are concerned as $129 billion in crypto moves annually, with 50 billion rubles exchanged daily. Calls for market regulation.
data.description: The deputy finance minister of Russia has stated that approximately 50 billion rubles is exchanged daily in the crypto market. He emphasized the need for regulation in the cryptocurrency space to manage this significant amount of transactions.

data.title: EfTEN United Property Fund net profit decreased to 223k euros in January. NAV was 12.85 euros, increasing 0.8% monthly.
data.description: In January, EfTEN United Property Fund recorded a net profit of 223,000 euros, a decrease from 408,000 euros in the same period the previous year. This decline was mainly attributed to the investment in EfTEN Real Estate Fund AS shares, which saw a 2.9% increase in share price on the Tallinn Stock Exchange in January 2026, compared to a 6.1% increase in January 2025. The net asset value (NAV) per fund unit stood at 12.85 euros at the end of January, reflecting a monthly increase of 0.8%.

data.title: Roche reports positive phase III results for Gazyva/Gazyvaro in primary membranous nephropathy, achieving statistically significant complete remission at 2 years; no new safety concerns emerged.
data.description: In Basel on February 16, 2026, Roche revealed that in the phase III MAJESTY study involving adults with primary membranous nephropathy, the primary endpoint was successfully met by Gazyva®/Gazyvaro® (obinutuzumab). The results displayed statistically significant and clinically meaningful outcomes, indicating that a higher number of individuals achieved complete remission at two years (104 weeks) with Gazyva/Gazyvaro as compared to tacrolimus. The safety profile of Gazyva/Gazyvaro remained consistent with its established characteristics, with no new safety concerns identified during the study. The stocks associated with Roche are listed as SIX: RO and ROG on the Swiss Stock Exchange, as well as OTCQX: RHHBY.

data.title: Infortar acquired its own shares on Nasdaq Tallinn Stock Exchange from 9-13 February 2026.
data.description: During the period of February 9 to 13, 2026, Aktsiaselts Infortar purchased its own shares on the Nasdaq Tallinn Stock Exchange.

data.title: Study by Beyfortus in The Lancet Infectious Diseases reveals benefits for infants post-first RSV season.
data.description: A recent research study by Beyfortus, as reported in The Lancet Infectious Diseases, indicates positive outcomes for infants extending beyond their initial respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) season. The study highlights significant advantages for infants in subsequent RSV seasons.
